commits: d092496 fix(economy): FuelEmpty fails Accepted/CargoLoaded contracts too — releases the deadhead-stranded escrow (jumpgate-2c0c2d92bb)
build.tests_observed: TDD failing run: `cargo test -p jumpgate-core fuel_empty_mid_deadhead` → FAILED, panic at 'Accepted contract reached Failed within the step bound' (contract stayed Accepted, escrow locked — exactly the plan's predicted failure). After fix: same command → `test economy::tests::fuel_empty_mid_deadhead_refunds_escrow ... ok`. Full suite `cargo test -p jumpgate-core` → 226 passed; 0 failed; 2 ignored (lib) + 7 passed; 0 failed + 6 passed; 0 failed + 0 doc-tests — all ok. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` → Finished clean (jumpgate-core, jumpgate-commons-cut, jumpgate-py), zero warnings.
deviations: Arm 2 (CargoLoaded) of the test invokes resolve_failures directly after the post-load direct-field-write fuel drain, instead of stepping the world to FuelEmpty: World::step's stage-1c dispatch promotes CargoLoaded→InTransit BEFORE stage-3 FuelEmpty detection, so the plan's literal sequence (load, drain, step) can never present a CargoLoaded contract to stage 3c; the in-window failure only occurs when FuelEmpty fires in the load tick itself, and the direct stage call reproduces exactly that stage-3c input. Minimal faithful correction; noted in the test comment and commit body. Arm 1 (Accepted/deadhead) runs fully end-to-end through World::step.

The world.rs two_body_starved_contract_fixture is private to world.rs's test module, so its shape was rebuilt in economy.rs's test module as starved_two_body_contract_fixture(from_station_index, to_station_index) — parameterized by contract direction so one fixture serves both arms (origin-far = deadhead arm; origin-co-located = load arm). This is what the plan's 'build the existing ... shape' instructs; recorded for completeness.

Beyond the plan's named edit sites, the stale doc-comment lines on resolve_failures ('For each InTransit contract...', 'status InTransit -> Failed') and the FailureCause::FuelEmpty variant doc ('only an InTransit contract can fail') were updated to describe the widened three-status behavior — comment-only accuracy fixes on the exact functions the plan modifies (FailureCause is in the plan's Files list).

The plan's filigree commands were insufficient as written: start-work requires --assignee (added: claude), and bug workflow does not allow fixing→closed — it requires fixing→verifying plus a required fix_verification field before close. Walked: start-work --assignee claude --advance (triage→confirmed→fixing), update --status verifying, update -f fix_verification=<test evidence>, then close with reason. Issue jumpgate-2c0c2d92bb now Status: closed.
build.notes: Goldens verified unchanged at HEAD (grep): HASH_FORMAT_VERSION = 4 (hash.rs:108), GOLDEN_ZERO_STATE_HASH = 0xafdc_5c35_6266_0ff0 (hash.rs:114), zero-world state_hash golden 0xa29b_6334_16f7_cd20 (hash.rs:947), GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH = 0x1798_b108_edae_5bb6 (config.rs:661). Commit d092496 touches exactly one file (crates/jumpgate-core/src/economy.rs, 258 insertions / 5 deletions), nothing forbidden staged (.gitignore remains modified-unstaged; runs/ untracked). Fix detail: resolve_failures now matches Accepted | CargoLoaded | InTransit (was != InTransit → continue); settle_contract_failure's FuelEmpty debug_assert arm widened to the same three statuses with a jumpgate-2c0c2d92bb citation; settle body untouched (it already handles cargo == None: refund leg + hauler release, sink leg skipped). The band has zero FuelEmpty events so scenario_trophic runs are bit-identical by construction (noted in the commit body per Step 1.4). filigree jumpgate-2c0c2d92bb closed with fix_verification recorded. Next task (Task 2) is the heterogeneity-metric recalibration in diagnostics.rs, filigree jumpgate-50c6a8a3bd.

commits: d41ed82 lab(pirates): recalibrate risk-heterogeneity against labeled runs; new hungry-roamer positive control (jumpgate-50c6a8a3bd)
build.tests_observed: TDD red: `cargo test -p jumpgate-core diagnostics` -> sparse_clumped_minority_routes_read_heterogeneous FAILED under the old formula (panic 'robs clumped on 2 of 9 occupied routes at 1-3 robs/window must read heterogeneous'), 6 passed 1 failed. After fit: same command -> 7 passed; 0 failed. Full suite `cargo test -p jumpgate-core` -> 227 passed; 0 failed; 2 ignored (lib) + 7 passed + 6 passed + 0 doc-tests, all ok. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> Finished clean (jumpgate-core, jumpgate-commons-cut, jumpgate-py), zero warnings. Labeled-set re-run (release, 50k ticks): baseline seeds 7/23/42/99 all verdict=Alive risk_heterogeneous=true (seed 7 flipped from the RiskEqualized misread); hungry-roamer control seeds 7/23 both RiskEqualized (instrument-kill intact, 2/2). World trajectories bit-identical pre/post fit (identical robs/trips/HHI evidence per seed - classify() is downstream of the world).
deviations: Step 2.2: the plan's pre-identified RUN-AGGREGATE-HHI candidate was MEASURED NON-SEPARATING (raw 130-153 clumped vs 123-143 equalized; active-normalized 548-714 vs 551-629 - overlapping both ways) and was NOT adopted. The STOP clause did not apply because formulas DO separate with margin on two independent axes; per the governing directive ('choose the smallest metric change that classifies ALL labeled runs correctly with margin') the fit kept the existing per-window normalized HHI formula and persistence clause structure: HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI 600->2204 (measured margin midpoint of clumped-min 2918 / equalized-max 1490; the old 600 sat below BOTH labels and never discriminated) plus a new HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES=3 on the kept persistence clause (measured hot-change excess: clumped max +1 - seed 7's 12v11 was the sole misread cause - vs equalized min +5; midpoint 3). Both constants documented with the measured table in diagnostics.rs.

Step 2.3: cycling_heterogeneous synthetic actives changed 1/3 -> 2/4 (mean normalized HHI 2000 -> 3000) because the re-derived threshold 2204 exceeds the old series' 2000; intent ('clumped persistent robs') preserved, explicitly allowed by the plan ('update ONLY as far as the formula change requires').

Step 2.5: also updated the trophic_run.rs header doc-comment (file not in the plan's Files list) - it documents the old positive-control recipe and the step's heading says 'everywhere it is documented'; comment-only, hash-neutral.
Step 2.5: corrected the sweep_trophic.py panel comment that claimed the RUN-AGGREGATE HHI is 'the sparsity-robust read' - falsified by this task's measurement; comment-only, in a plan-listed file.
Step 2.6: filigree start-work required --assignee (added: claude); task workflow allowed in_progress -> closed directly (no verifying hop needed, unlike Task 1's bug workflow).
build.notes: RECORDED MEASUREMENTS (Step 2.1, 50k-tick release runs; per-window-HHI = unnormalized mean / NORM = active-pirate-normalized mean, the metric's input): baseline s7 TRUE-clumped: old-verdict RiskEqualized(WRONG) het=false, per-window-HHI 676, NORM 3070, RUN-AGG-HHI 130 (norm 548), routes-robbed 9, hot/traffic changes 12/11, robs 39; s23 TRUE-clumped: Alive true, 614, NORM 2962, AGG 153 (714), routes 12, 13/14, robs 66; s42 TRUE-clumped: Alive true, 711, NORM 2918, AGG 140 (560), routes 10, 10/13, robs 41; s99 TRUE-clumped: Alive true, 815, NORM 3498, AGG 134 (569), routes 9, 7/13, robs 35; control s7 TRUE-equalized: RiskEqualized false, 334, NORM 1490, AGG 143 (629), routes 8, 22/16, robs 137; control s23 TRUE-equalized: RiskEqualized false, 317, NORM 1472, AGG 123 (551), routes 11, 21/16, robs 139. FITTED: HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI=2204, HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES=3 (diagnostics.rs). POST-FIT: all 6 labels correct; fuel_empty 0 everywhere. Goldens untouched at HEAD: HASH_FORMAT_VERSION=4 (hash.rs:108), GOLDEN_ZERO_STATE_HASH=0xafdc_5c35_6266_0ff0 (hash.rs:114), zero-world state_hash 0xa29b_6334_16f7_cd20 (hash.rs:947), GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH=0x1798_b108_edae_5bb6 (config.rs:661). filigree jumpgate-50c6a8a3bd CLOSED with the fitted numbers. Step 2.7 BAKED (bench, untracked - runs/ never staged): /home/john/jumpgate/runs/media_baseline/s7.{jsonl,out} and s23.{jsonl,out} (25 JSONL windows + 29 stdout lines each); RESULT s7: Alive cycled=true risk_heterogeneous=true outcomes_disperse=true fuel_empty=0 robs=39 laden_trips=709 purchases=49; RESULT s23: Alive true/true/true fuel_empty=0 robs=66 laden_trips=699 purchases=49 - these are the Task-9 cross-branch behavior-identity references (media-off must reproduce byte-for-byte on JSONL and every stdout line except the trailing MEDIA line Task 8 adds). NEXT TASK NOTE (Task 3, RngStream::Media): baseline default knobset in sweep_trophic.py now carries the 4-knob control string; trophic_run header documents the hungry-roamer recipe.

(4) Plan steps real: measured calibration table documented in diagnostics.rs (HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI=2204, HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES=3, both with the labeled-run numbers); sweep_trophic.py default control knobset = pirate_max_reach_au=999,stay_milli=0,upkeep_per_tick=200,grubstake_micros=2000000000 with rationale; spec section 9 hungry-roamer text present; the falsified 'sparsity-robust' sweep comment removed; runs/media_baseline/s7+s23 .jsonl(25 lines)/.out(29 lines) baked; filigree jumpgate-50c6a8a3bd status=closed with fitted numbers in close_reason. commits: 47e9555 feat(media): RngStream::Media + SALT_MEDIA (frac sqrt3) + pinned first-draw golden
build.tests_observed: TDD red (Step 3.2): `cargo test -p jumpgate-core rng` -> compile FAILED, E0599 'no variant or associated item named Media found for enum rng::RngStream' at all 6 test use sites. After implementation with placeholder-0 golden (Step 3.4): same command -> golden_first_draws_are_pinned FAILED with panic 'Media[master=0] first draw drifted: left: 5222731996269755031, right: 0' (6 passed; 1 failed). Pasted the captured value (hex 0x487a_df14_52b9_ba97) into the assert; re-run -> 7 rng tests ok (0 failed). Full suite `cargo test -p jumpgate-core` -> 228 passed; 0 failed; 2 ignored (lib) + 7 passed; 0 failed + 6 passed; 0 failed + 0 doc-tests, all ok (was 227 lib tests at Task-2 HEAD; +1 = the new media test). `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> Finished clean (jumpgate-core, jumpgate-commons-cut, jumpgate-py), zero warnings.
deviations: The plan's existing-test inventory implied golden_first_draws_are_pinned already had a Piracy arm; in reality it pins only Intervention and Scenario. The Media arm was added exactly as Step 3.1/3.4 specify; the pre-existing arms were left untouched. No scope change.

The golden-capture placeholder assert (Step 3.4) was written together with the Step 3.1 tests rather than after Step 3.3, so the Step 3.2 red run was a compile failure (E0599, no variant) and the runtime golden failure surfaced immediately after implementation. The capture discipline itself was followed exactly: value taken from the observed failure message (5222731996269755031 = 0x487a_df14_52b9_ba97), never invented.

No filigree issue exists for Task 3 (searched: 0 results) and the plan section names none, so no tracker transitions were performed. Note: filigree CLI emitted 'warning: ACTOR_MISMATCH claimed=claude verified=john' when passing --actor claude in this environment.
build.notes: RECORDED GOLDEN (captured, Step 3.4): Media[master=0] first draw = 0x487a_df14_52b9_ba97 (decimal 5222731996269755031) against rand_chacha 0.10.0 / rand_core 0.10.1. SALT_MEDIA = 0xBB67_AE85_84CA_A73B (64-bit fractional bits of sqrt(3), the SHA-512 h1 constant — continues SALT_PIRACY 0x6A09_E667_F3BC_C908 = frac sqrt(2) convention; doc-commented in rng.rs). Commit 47e9555 touches exactly one file: /home/john/jumpgate/crates/jumpgate-core/src/rng.rs (48 insertions, 2 deletions); nothing forbidden staged (.gitignore remains modified-unstaged; runs/ untracked). Next task (Task 4, MediaCfg config-shape golden) is the first of the two single-cause golden commits: GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH moves there, re-derived via the ignored print_golden_config test (config.rs:931 prints it).

Plan conformance (Steps 3.1-3.5) read against the actual diff: Media variant APPENDED (existing variants unreordered), SALT_MEDIA=0xBB67_AE85_84CA_A73B doc-commented as frac sqrt(3) continuing the SALT_PIRACY frac-sqrt(2) convention, media field seeded master^salt in from_master, arms added to salt() and stream(), distinct_streams_differ + golden_first_draws_are_pinned extended with Media arms, drain test drains BOTH Scenario and Piracy (1000 draws each) before asserting Media unperturbed — all exactly as the plan specifies. Adversarial spot-checks: (a) the pinned golden 0x487a_df14_52b9_ba97 cannot be fabricated — golden_first_draws_are_pinned computes the first draw live from ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(0 ^ SALT_MEDIA) and asserts equality, and it passed under my own run; decimal equivalence confirmed (printf -> 5222731996269755031).
